message: No test pins the README security claims this contract exists to protect: the exact executor command `codex exec --json --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`, the ACP adapter path `npx -y @zed-industries/codex-acp@latest`, and the 'unsandboxed' warning appear in README.md:54-77 but in neither requiredDocMentions nor requiredSecurityPolicyMentions, and plain `codex exec` cannot be a forbidden phrase (substring of the full command). The goal's stated problem — README drifting back to plain `codex exec` while docs/agents.md stays correct — would not be caught by any test. Adding the full command string to requiredDocMentions would close most of the gap.
evidence: Verified internal/docs/docs_test.go in full: requiredDocMentions (line 66) and requiredSecurityPolicyMentions (line 134) contain no 'codex exec', 'dangerously', 'npx', or 'unsandboxed' entries. README.md lines 54-55 and 76-77 carry the claims unpinned. Contract AC #9 only required SECURITY.md pins, so non-blocking.

message: SECURITY.md says `pactum execute plan` lets users inspect the exact command Pactum would launch, but under the default ACP transport the plan records the CLI descriptor while `execute run` launches the external `npx` ACP adapter.
evidence: ACP is selected by default in internal/app/app.go:239-240; dry-run plans are built from the CLI descriptor in internal/agents/dryrun.go:11-12; ACP runs launch npx adapters in internal/agents/acp_transport.go:191 and :193.

message: README.md says the generated default registers an unpinned `claude` entry, but the default config pins `model: claude-opus-4-8` and docs/agents.md states every registry entry pins its model.
evidence: internal/app/config.go:99-105 creates the default claude entry with Model `claude-opus-4-8`; docs/agents.md:65-69 documents the pinned default. The stale README wording existed before this diff, so it is advisory.

message: The SECURITY.md concept assertions do not pin the required safe-usage guidance to review, pin, or control external ACP/CLI tooling in restricted environments.
evidence: requiredSecurityPolicyMentions checks trusted repos, execute plan, path scope, credentials, environment, reporting URL, and main-only support, but no ACP/CLI/npx/external-tooling review or pinning term. Removing SECURITY.md's 'Control the external tooling' section would not fail this test.
